programmes.show_description: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, as Jon Richardson and Judi Love take on Rob Beckett and Katherine Parkinson. Spencer Jones is in Dictionary Corner.
There's a singing star this time and a sax player too. A couple of yachties. And an ex-copper from Essex seeks an alpha male fit to be a king.
Juicy Box: Gordon helps a Haitian juice bar and restaurant in Brooklyn. Jacques opened Juicy Box to provide healthier options for the neighbourhood, but it's fallen into chaos.
Sun, sea and saving money? Yes please! Jasmine Harman reveals industry tricks to indulgent beach getaways for less, from the inside scoop on resorts to cutting costs of package trips. (Ep5)
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Fiat Uno Turbo: In Wellingborough, Tim and Fuzz fix up one of the fastest hatchbacks of the 80s. Owner Gerry's ill health has meant that his Uno has been left to rot on his drive.
Grudge Match: It's Frank versus Mike as the guys take to the track for a grudge match drag race to find out who has the fastest ride.
In Bradford, Jason plans a menu of Yorkshire's finest but keeps throwing his efforts in the bin! After postcode palaver, have Jason's chances 'bin' and gone? (Ep2/5)
Steve and Andrew host the week's second visit at Cae Court Hotel in Bridgend, where the B&Bers raise a tostada to a brilliant Spanish brekkie.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: There are build problems at Stretch and Rachael's Portuguese reno. Can David and Lane sniff out more business at their French emporium? Will Rachel save the Spanish ranch?
Laura Hamilton helps sisters Sheila and Jean to look for a holiday home on Spain's Mijas Costa for all their family and friends to enjoy, for £120k. Will they find their dream place?
Leah Charles-King helps Sarah and Andy find the 'pawfect' place for their whole family - including their beloved dog Ruby - on Spain's southern Costa Blanca, for their budget of £160k.
Kingston upon Thames. Basketballer Ken, who claims to be the best cook in London, themes his night around his favourite sport, with 'slam dunk salmon' and a sportswear dress code. (Ep1/5)
Kingston upon Thames. It's all hands on deck for interior designer Erika's pirate party aboard her boat, on a night featuring pole-dancing, a Titanic moment and ridiculous rapping. (Ep2/5)
Kingston upon Thames. Super-organised Sarah hosts a 'Roaring 20s' night, with 'dolled-up' prawns and 'swanky' lamb, on an evening featuring revelations, romantic poetry and dancing. (Ep3/5)
Kingston upon Thames. Photographer Jamie plans an evening of old-skool classics, with an ambitious menu and a superstar DJ set. (Ep4/5)
Kingston upon Thames. Retired golfer David goes all-out American, from Louisiana seafood chowder to a bucking bronco. But there's chaos when his cat breaks dinner party etiquette. (Ep5/5)
Danni Menzies helps motorbike lovers Gary and Lynn look for their dream holiday home in France's stunning Haute-Vienne region. Will any of Danni's options get them revved up?
Kirstie and Phil help two couples who are hoping to relocate to Surrey, one of the most expensive counties in the country, from a forever family home to a total lifestyle change.
...Alice Roberts: Alice discovers ancient gems in Parma. In Turin, she learns all about Roman bread, before catching a train up and across the Alps with Hannibal on her mind... (Ep4)
The Dog House's long-term residents need new homes. Can inseparable Chihuahua trio Rio, Prince and Blue Boy, and double trouble George and Daisy, find their forever humans?
Richard, who's 35, is rushed to Queen's in Nottingham. He collapsed suddenly at home and may be going into organ failure. And Colin's sliced his hand open with a Stanley knife.
...Protocol: (2011) Part four of the Tom Cruise action-thriller franchise. The Impossible Missions Force fight to clear their name, while also trying to avert nuclear war. Busy, busy, busy!
(2013) Sci-fi thriller. A young programmer agrees to help a reclusive genius to test whether his lifelike android has consciousness, with dramatic results.
Brighton's night police patrol the town's hectic night life - from a possible suicide up on the cliff, to an urgent manhunt for a violent assailant with a crowbar.
Richard, who's 35, is rushed to Queen's in Nottingham. He collapsed suddenly at home and may be going into organ failure. And Colin's sliced his hand open with a Stanley knife.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
VW Corrado: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue Ashley's 1992 VW Corrado, which has been rotting away since he fell ill. Can the boys bring some joy back to Ashley's life?
Escape to Motor Mountain: The guys find a mountain of motorcycles, but can they get through the avalanche of booby traps? Plus: a stubborn collector and a warehouse mega-pick.
In Bradford, Gaby hopes that her Sardinian soirée, singing son, cocktails, party tricks and dolce vita dinner will sweeten the deal with her guests. (Ep3/5)
The B&Bers visit Shaun and Sarah's glampsite in Aberystwyth, where a pair of resident diddy donkeys steal the show - and plenty of hearts.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Rachel and her brother open their ranch for guests. David and Lane take drastic action at their emporium. Stretch and Rachael finish their renovation.
Sarah and Ian met in Spain and now want a holiday home in south-east Lanzarote for themselves and their family to enjoy. Jean Johansson shows them five great finds.
Antonia and Ross love Ibiza. They've holidayed there many times, and now hope to find their own holiday home there. Can Lucy Alexander deliver their dream?
Milton Keynes. The first host, Ray Dodd, finds his guests hard to please. With his starter rejected and a main returned, he's left sweating over his chances of winning the prize... (Ep1/5)
Milton Keynes. Michelle's black-tie evening doesn't go to plan, as her guests diss her food and call the main course 'poison'. Will her luck turn with the Monte Carlo entertainment? (Ep2/5)
Milton Keynes. Joke-loving Stuart offers his guests a flavour of the Serengeti. Can his exotic menu and parlour games smooth over the insults and tensions of the previous nights? (Ep3/5)
Milton Keynes. Choir director Joseph gives his guests a warm welcome, as he serves Ghanaian dishes of chicken liver pâté and groundnut soup with coconut rice, and a musical treat. (Ep4/5)
Milton Keynes. After a tension-filled week, country lover Susan is confident that she can bring the group together with her back-to-basics French food and origami masterclass. (Ep5/5)
Sandy and Andy look for a new home near the beautiful lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy. Can Danni Menzies find their dream traditional-style house with plenty of space for their £70k?
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon in Derbyshire and their oldest ever house-hunters, Margot and Henry, in Berkshire.
In Radcliffe-on-Trent, Joel and Nicola restore Joel's grandparents' 1970s Victorian cottage and add a plush extension, with a stretched budget and a tight Yuletide timeline.

programmes.show_name: Nando's vs KFC: Battle of the...
programmes.show_description: ...Brands: KFC and Nando's go head-to-head as Kate Quilton explores the battle for our taste buds in a fiery look at flavour, fandom and the billion-pound fight to win the chicken wars.
Could you survive being handcuffed to a total stranger 24/7? Jonathan Ross challenges 18 brave Brits to stay together, as they compete to win a £100k prize. (Ep1)
Could you stay handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? Self-confessed prude Charlie is hooked to heavily tattooed Rob. Can Christian hairdresser Nina handle Sara's chatty nature? (Ep2)
This time it's Corrie, The Stolen Girl, The Piano, Mickey Rourke on Celebrity Big Brother, savoury cocktails on Saturday Kitchen, the News on Katy Perry in space, and more.
